headline: Nvidia Stock Was a Reliable Outperformer. Why That’s Changed.
summary: Nvidia  stock rose roughly 300% from the end of 2022—when the artificial intelligence revolution began—to its recent peak in October, more than tripling the broader market’s advance.  Nvidia’s  shares are in this position because of a powerful combination of factors.  First of all, Nvidia’s general outperformance had become so extreme that investors became willing to take winnings by selling the stock in some recent periods.

headline: AMD EPYC Powers AI Agent Workloads As CPUs Gain Data Center Share
summary: AMD EPYC CPUs are seeing stronger use in AI "agentic" workloads as hyperscalers increasingly rely on CPU compute alongside GPUs. Large cloud providers such as Amazon and Microsoft are reported to be adopting high core count EPYC chips for next generation AI agents and orchestration layers. AMD has reached over 41% of server CPU revenue share, with EPYC gaining traction in premium data center sockets tied to AI infrastructure.
